meta.title: dorm, somn

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: dorm, somn
root.mentor_note: dorm and somn are two Latin roots for the same idea, and English keeps both of them busy.

attempt.direction_prompt: Wide awake and active, or lying dormant, as if asleep?

context_sentences: The volcano had lain dormant for four hundred years before the first tremors returned.
Her old talent for painting stayed dormant for two decades until retirement finally freed up the time.
Months of insomnia finally sent her to a specialist who diagnosed the real cause within a week.
Deadline season always brought his insomnia back, night after night of watching the ceiling.
The heavy lunch left the whole meeting room somnolent by half past two.
A somnolent Sunday afternoon was exactly what the whole family needed after the busy week.

construct_options.text: Wide awake and unable to fall asleep

context_sentences: As a child he was a committed somnambulist, once found asleep at the kitchen table at 3am.
The hotel had a quiet policy for somnambulists among its guests: leave doors gently blocked, never locked.

construct_options.text: A person who walks around while still asleep, a sleepwalker
A person who suffers from a persistent inability to sleep
A person who talks constantly and cannot stay quiet
